principle: If a component does not help answer (1) what is broken? (2) why is it broken? (3) what should I fix first? (4) what changed? (5) can I trust this? — remove or move it.

removed_or_moved_elements.element: Free-form 'Support / Candidate' text rows under the Simple 3LS Flowchart
removed_or_moved_elements.reason: Listed candidate labels (H01, RFX, MET, METS) as if they were system rows. Replaced with the Decision Layer projection in its own tab so candidate labels never appear as system rows on the Overview.
removed_or_moved_elements.preserved_in: Top 3 panel (text-only when non-registry); Roadmap tab

removed_or_moved_elements.element: Verbose per-bundle steps in the Leverage Queue
removed_or_moved_elements.reason: Steps repeat what the Roadmap tab already shows in full. Queue card now shows one short summary plus dependency_count.

removed_or_moved_elements.element: Repeated source text on every node card
removed_or_moved_elements.reason: Node cards previously surfaced source paths and schema paths inline; this duplicated content also lives in the inspector. Detail moved to inspector + Diagnostics replay panel only.

removed_or_moved_elements.element: Non-registry node handling on the graph
removed_or_moved_elements.reason: Old behavior allowed non-registry labels (e.g. H01) to render as nodes in some modes. New behavior rejects them at builder time and surfaces a single warning in graph.warnings; UI exposes nothing for the rejected node.

tests_proving_no_loss_of_critical_warnin: __tests__/components/Met03Panels.test.tsx: keeps overview concise with only four operator panels — top3-warning and queue-warning still surface in fail-closed state
__tests__/components/DashboardPage.test.tsx: top 3 + queue + flow + tabs render after simplification
__tests__/lib/topThreeRecommendations.test.ts: stale, missing, invalid, partial — all warnings still surface
__tests__/lib/registryContract.test.ts: graph rejects unknown / future / demoted nodes
remaining_risks: Decision Layer view duplicates some content visible in Overview B (canonical loop). Acceptable: Decision Layer adds the policy/enforcement axis the Overview lacks.
